title: ricinocarpus anisodontus
intro: Ricinocarpus anisodontus, a plant species native to tropical Africa, is also known as the African blood root due to its reddish-brown taproot. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. It has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its ability to stimulate blood circulation and promote detoxification. In traditional herbal preparations, Ricinocarpus anisodontus is often dried and crushed into powder, or made into a decoction by infusing the roots in hot water.
